title: fix: remove Myceli proxy routing
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Deletes the obsolete `pollinations-myceli-proxy` Worker package and stale proxy migration docs.
- Updates app deploy cutover to attach `<sub>.pollinations.ai` directly to the Myceli Pages project using Myceli credentials only.
- Removes old-account deploy secrets from the app deploy workflow.
- Adds direct `kpi.pollinations.ai` routing to the KPI Worker and removes proxy wording from the old economics dashboard docs.
- Updates forwarded-header comments/tests to describe a generic trusted edge hop instead of the deleted proxy Worker.

Validation:
- `node --check apps/_scripts/deploy-app.js`
- `bash -n apps/_scripts/deploy.sh`
- `biome check --write apps/_scripts/deploy-app.js shared/public-origin.ts shared/client-ip.ts gen.pollinations.ai/test/proxy-client-ip.test.ts`
- `prettier --check .github/workflows/app-deploy-automatic.yml`
- `npx vitest run test/proxy-client-ip.test.ts` in `gen.pollinations.ai`
- `npm run build` and `npx wrangler deploy --dry-run` in `apps/operation/kpi`

Notes:
- `gh` is not installed on this machine, so the PR was created through the GitHub connector.
- Lefthook was not available in PATH during commit; the targeted checks above were run manually.

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the proxy
gist.blurb: We've retired the old Myceli proxy worker and streamlined our app deployment routing. Fewer hops, cleaner code!
gist.summary: Removed the obsolete `pollinations-myceli-proxy` Worker and updated deployment scripts to attach subdomains directly to the Myceli Pages project.
gist.impact: Internal routing is now simpler and more direct, reducing infrastructure complexity for app deployments.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as an electrician/engineer, bypassing old tangled wires (the proxy) to connect a direct, glowing wire between two servers. The robot helper carrying away a box of old parts.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, connecting a glowing lime green (#ecf874) wire directly between two retro servers. A chunky monitor-head robot happily carries away a cardboard box labeled 'PROXY'. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
